observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to properly record the purchases of welfare goods under the account Welfare Goods for Distribution and the distributed portion to Welfare Goods Expenses for proper monitoring of receipts, issuances, and balances of goods procured out of the current year’s balances of LDRRMF, 20 per cent Development Fund and Bayanihan Grant.
observations.reason: Not Reiterated in Observation No. 2 of Part II of the Report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor create an Inventory Committee
composed of adequate number of members to be able to complete the physical
inventory of the PPE in three months or less, and who shall be temporarily
relieved of all their regular duties to devote their full time in the conduct of the
physical inventory taking until the same is completed, in compliance with COA
Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020. After which, it is recommended that
the Municipal Mayor direct the:
a. Inventory Committee to prepare a Physical Inventory Plan and accordingly
conduct a comprehensive physical inventory of all PPE of the Municipality and
prepare the corresponding Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant
and Equipment (RPCPPE) following the guidelines set forth in said COA
Circular; and
b. General Services Officer Designate and Municipal Accountant to maintain their
respective property records.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to
use the inventory account to record the purchase of supplies and materials except
when they are for immediate use or distribution and not for stock, in which case
the supplies may be charged directly to the appropriate expense accounts.
We further recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the GSO Designate to
prepare the Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI) and submit it to the
Municipal Accountant who, on the basis thereof, shall properly record the
expended portions of supplies and materials.

We recommended that the Municipality Mayor direct:
a. the MDRRMC to identify and incorporate, under a separate caption, in the
correct format of LDRRMFIP, the specific projects and activities to be charged
against the unexpended previous years’ LDRRMF;
b. all concerned officials to ensure that all projects and programs in the
LDRRMFIP are fully implemented.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant and
Disbursing Officer to adhere strictly to the provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002
in the granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor ensure that the JO contracts are executed
between her and the Job Order workers indicating therein the specific services to
be rendered by the latter, the office where they will be assigned and their
immediate supervisors, service duration and other terms and conditions as
applicable.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to
return to the Bureau of Treasury the balances of the fund transfers from various
NGAs that had been dormant for more than five years or the purpose of which had
already been achieved by the Municipality.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Budget Officer
and Municipal Accountant to consider charging against the SEF the salaries of
GEA NHS teachers.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to see
to it that all transactions of the Municipality are properly recorded and the
accounts used are in accordance with COA Circular No. 2015-009.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct all members of the GAD Focal
Point System to undergo trainings on HGDG to build their competence in the
conduct of gender analysis of programs and projects for purposes of GAD budget
attribution.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Social Worker
Development Officer (MSWDO) and Municipal Treasurer
a) to act in accordance with the existing guidelines to ensure efficient
and effective way of releasing the financial assistance; and
b) to provide the Audit Team with proofs that the financial assistance
intended for the 32 households from the 9 barangays of the
Municipality amounting to P83,000.00 were actually received by the
intended beneficiaries, otherwise, the amount must be returned by the
concerned barangays and consequently be returned by the
Municipality to the source agency.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer and Accountant to review and reconcile their prior years’ records of RPT/SET Receivable accounts, particularly focusing on the differences noted as of January 1, 2018, to ensure that the deficiencies or errors will not anymore be carried over to succeeding calendar years. Henceforth, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer to continue regularly reconcile their respective records to ensure the reliability of the balances RPT and SET Receivable accounts as of year-end.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor had directed the Municipal Treasurer and Accountant to review and reconcile their prior years’ records of RPT/SET Receivable accounts, particularly focusing on the differences noted as of January 1, 2018, to ensure that the deficiencies or errors will not anymore be carried over to succeeding calendar years. The Updated Real Property Tax (RPT) and Special Education Tax Receivables per barangays were already submitted to Auditor.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer/General Services Office Designate to analyze and reconcile the discrepancy between the Accounting records and RPCPPE and make the necessary adjustments.
observations.managementAction: The Office of the Municipal Accountant had already effected the necessary adjustment/accounting entry to reconcile the discrepancy in the amount of ₱1,114,848.00 in the accounting records of Property, Plant and Equipment.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the MSWDO to coordinate with concerned Barangay officials to facilitate the submission to the COA of the updated contact numbers of beneficiaries, for the Audit Team’s further or expanded confirmation.
observations.managementAction: The Office of the Municipal Social Welfare and Development Office (MSWDO) had submitted the updated contact numbers of SAP beneficiaries for further or expanded confirmation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Budget Officer, Municipal Accountant and other concerned Municipal officials to submit explanation/s on why the payments of honoraria to the members of the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC), Technical Working Group (TWG) and BAC Secretariat were charged against the General Fund and exceeded the proceeds from sale of bid documents totaling P154,500.00. Further, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to treat as trust receipts the proceeds from the sale of bid documents instead of Other Service Income in the General Fund and to ensure that payments of honoraria for procurement-related activities do not exceed the said collections.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant treated as trust receipts the proceeds from the sale of bid documents instead of Other Service Income in the General Fund.
observations.reason: Explanations were submitted on why the payments of honoraria to the members of the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC), Technical Working Group (TWG) and BAC Secretariat were charged against the General Fund and exceeded the proceeds from sale of bid documents totaling P154,500.00.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Bids and Awards Committee to explain to the Audit Team, for further evaluation, why various procurement of the Municipality made in favor of GAMECO should not be suspended or disallowed in audit due to violation of the conflict of interest provisions of GPPB Circular No. 08-2017.
observations.managementAction: Upon learning of the guidelines the municipality had stopped procurements in favor of GAMECO.
observations.reason: The explanations given were accepted by the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the BAC Secretariat to submit to the Audit Team the justifications on its inability to comply with the posting requirement under GPPB Circular No. 01-2020, for further evaluation.
observations.managementAction: Management justified that due to COVID-19 toxicity they failed to post the said procurements in Philippine Government Electronic Procurement System (PhilGEPS). The explanations given were accepted by the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the General Service Officer to submit to the Auditor samples/photographs and the amended Acceptance and Inspection Reports indicating therein the complete specifications and brand names, to facilitate price verification and technical review of the reasonableness of the contract costs.
observations.managementAction: Samples/photographs and the amended Acceptance and Inspection Reports indicating therein the complete specifications and brand names were submitted to the Audit Team.
